title: fagus sylvatica f. atropunicea
intro: Fagus sylvatica f. atropunicea, also known as the Copperbeech, is a cultivar of the European Beech tree, characterized by its copper-colored leaves. The Copperbeech has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help treat various health issues. The health benefits of the Copperbeech include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. The herbal preparations of the Copperbeech include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
